# Deployment Requirements Checklist: GCP Deployment for AISoftware Studio MVP

**Purpose**: Validate deployment requirement quality, clarity, completeness, and consistency before tasks or implementation proceed.
**Created**: 2026-07-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md), [plan.md](../plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are deployment requirements defined for both production services, including the backend `aisoftware-studio-api` and frontend `aisoftware-studio-web`? [Completeness, Spec Section FR-001, Spec Section FR-002, Plan Section Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK002 Are independent deployment requirements documented for frontend and backend so each service can be released without requiring the other except for intentional API/CORS coordination? [Completeness, Spec Section FR-016, Plan Section Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK003 Are the existing MVP scope boundaries explicitly preserved across spec and plan, including no database, authentication, CMS, admin panel, payment, queue, or persistent lead storage? [Completeness, Spec Section FR-017, Plan Section Constraints]
- [ ] CHK004 Are production-only deployment boundaries documented clearly enough that local development remains unchanged? [Completeness, Spec Section FR-020, Plan Section Constraints]
- [ ] CHK005 Are required deployment files named and located for backend, frontend, infra, scripts, docs, and README updates? [Completeness, Plan Section Files To Add, Plan Section Files To Update]
- [ ] CHK006 Are first-deployment documentation requirements complete enough for a clean GCP project, including APIs, auth, Artifact Registry, Secret Manager, Cloud Run, Cloud Build, and CORS setup? [Completeness, Spec Section FR-012, Plan Section Documentation Design]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is the default region `europe-central2` stated consistently while preserving an explicit override requirement for scripts and docs? [Clarity, Spec Section FR-023, Plan Section Target Platform]
- [ ] CHK008 Are Cloud Run service responsibilities described unambiguously for the backend and frontend, including service names, runtime role, and validation surface? [Clarity, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK009 Is the backend container port requirement specific enough to avoid hard-coded production ports and require listening on the Cloud Run `PORT` environment variable? [Clarity, Spec Section FR-003, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the frontend container port requirement specific enough to require Nginx on port `8080` and exclude Angular dev server usage in production? [Clarity, Spec Section FR-022, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK011 Are Angular SPA fallback requirements specific enough to cover direct route requests through the production static container? [Clarity, Spec Section FR-004, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK012 Is the production frontend API URL requirement clear about configurability and the prohibition on hard-coded real production URLs? [Clarity, Spec Section FR-005, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK013 Are Cloud Build requirements consistent across spec, plan, and contracts, including separate backend/frontend deployment files and a non-deploying PR checks file? [Consistency, Spec Section FR-009, Spec Section FR-010, Plan Section Cloud Build Design, Contracts Section Cloud Build Substitutions]
- [ ] CHK014 Are substitution and placeholder requirements consistent with the no-real-project-ID and no-secret constraints? [Consistency, Spec Section FR-011, Spec Section FR-018, Contracts Section Cloud Build Substitutions]
- [ ] CHK015 Are Secret Manager requirements consistent across spec, plan, contracts, and quickstart for `SMTP_PASSWORD` or equivalent sensitive values? [Consistency, Spec Section FR-008, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design, Contracts Section Deployment Interfaces]
- [ ] CHK016 Are CORS requirements consistent across spec and plan, including configurable allowed origins and no wildcard origins in production? [Consistency, Spec Section FR-006, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design, Plan Section Testing Strategy]
- [ ] CHK017 Are Cloud Run cost requirements consistent with the stated MVP posture, including min instances `0` by default for both services? [Consistency, Plan Section Performance Goals, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK018 Are custom domain requirements consistently framed as later manual documentation rather than automated infrastructure scope? [Consistency, Research Section Custom Domain, Plan Section Documentation Design]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK019 Are success criteria measurable for backend image build, local container health, frontend image build, SPA fallback, and Cloud Build configuration presence? [Measurability, Spec Section SC-002, Spec Section SC-003, Spec Section SC-004]
- [ ] CHK020 Are criteria for "no committed secrets" objective enough to review through repository inspection and placeholder rules? [Measurability, Spec Section SC-005, Plan Section Testing Strategy]
- [ ] CHK021 Are smoke-test requirements measurable enough to distinguish frontend availability, backend health, CORS behavior, and contact API behavior? [Measurability, Spec Section SC-008, Plan Section Testing Strategy]
- [ ] CHK022 Are local-development preservation criteria measurable enough to detect accidental production-only dependencies in local startup/test commands? [Measurability, Spec Section SC-012, Plan Section Constraints]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Are primary deployment scenarios covered for backend deploy, frontend deploy, safe production configuration, repeatable Cloud Build deployment, and visitor contact flow validation? [Coverage, Spec Section User Scenarios]
- [ ] CHK024 Are deployment-order requirements addressed for the case where backend CORS needs the frontend Cloud Run URL after the frontend is deployed? [Coverage, Spec Section Edge Cases, Quickstart Section 7]
- [ ] CHK025 Are preflight requirements documented for both local quality gates and script-based execution before deployment? [Coverage, Spec Section FR-015, Plan Section PowerShell Script Design]
- [ ] CHK026 Are rollback and troubleshooting requirements documented for release recovery, Cloud Run logs, common failures, secret rotation, CORS, and contact form issues? [Coverage, Spec Section FR-014, Plan Section Documentation Design]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK027 Are missing production environment variables and missing Secret Manager bindings represented as explicit edge cases or troubleshooting documentation requirements? [Edge Case, Spec Section Edge Cases, Plan Section Documentation Design]
- [ ] CHK028 Are missing GCP APIs, missing permissions, missing Artifact Registry repository, and missing secrets covered as operator-facing failure cases? [Edge Case, Spec Section Edge Cases, Plan Section PowerShell Script Design]
- [ ] CHK029 Are CORS failure modes covered for public frontend reachable but contact API blocked or rejected? [Edge Case, Spec Section Edge Cases, Plan Section Testing Strategy]
- [ ] CHK030 Are Docker context safety requirements specified to exclude `.env` files, service account JSON keys, caches, local test artifacts, and secrets? [Edge Case, Plan Section Files To Add]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK031 Are security requirements complete for Secret Manager use, non-sensitive env vars, no committed secrets, no service account keys, non-sensitive logging, and no wildcard production CORS? [Security, Spec Section FR-007, Spec Section FR-018, Spec Section FR-021]
- [ ] CHK032 Are production runtime requirements complete for avoiding development servers in backend and frontend containers? [Reliability, Spec Section FR-022, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK033 Are operational documentation requirements complete for first deployment, Cloud Build trigger setup, custom-domain notes, cost notes, smoke tests, and rollback? [Operations, Plan Section Documentation Design]
- [ ] CHK034 Are validation requirements complete for existing backend and frontend test commands so deployment work does not invalidate current quality gates? [Quality Gates, Plan Section Testing Strategy]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK035 Are GCP dependencies explicitly documented, including Cloud Run, Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, Secret Manager, IAM/API enablement, and `gcloud` CLI? [Dependency, Plan Section Primary Dependencies]
- [ ] CHK036 Are assumptions about production-only support, default region, Cloud Run min instances, and no Terraform/Pulumi stated consistently across planning artifacts? [Assumption, Spec Section Assumptions, Research Section Decision]
- [ ] CHK037 Are Artifact Registry image naming and repository substitution requirements sufficiently specified to avoid hard-coded private project values? [Dependency, Contracts Section Cloud Build Substitutions]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK038 Is the chosen frontend API URL configuration method defined tightly enough for tasks to implement without ambiguity, while allowing either build-time or deployment-time generated config only if tests/docs are updated? [Ambiguity, Plan Section Frontend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK039 Are Cloud Run environment variable names aligned with the backend settings model, or is any required mapping ambiguity called out for implementation tasks? [Ambiguity, Plan Section Backend Deployment Design]
- [ ] CHK040 Are references to production URLs, service names, secret names, and project IDs clearly separated between safe placeholders and operator-provided real values? [Ambiguity, Spec Section FR-011, Contracts Section Cloud Build Substitutions]

## Notes

- These items validate whether the requirements and planning artifacts are clear enough to implement and review.
- They do not validate whether Dockerfiles, Cloud Build YAML, scripts, or docs have already been implemented.
